summary: Iter17 (refactor to deps.py + daily overdue reminders) fully verified. 27/27 backend pytest cases pass, including refactor sanity on 14 GET endpoints, RBAC module gating via deps.require_module (cashier 403 on /clients, 200 on /payments, admin bypass), reminders status/run/logs endpoints, dedup on repeated /reminders/run, master toggle disable → run returns {disabled:true, scanned:0}, day-bucket boundary (only 7- and 9-day overdue contracts hit reminder_log; 6/8/10 skipped), and iter16 disbursement auto-record still fires on contract creation. Frontend Settings page renders the new RemindersCard with all 6 data-testids (reminders-card, reminders-run-now, reminders-toggle, reminders-last-run, reminders-next-run, reminders-sent-count, reminders-skipped-count); Run now triggers the API + toast + updates last-run timestamp; toggle off + Save persists reminders_enabled=false across reload. Scheduler log confirms daily_reminders CronTrigger(hour=0,minute=0 UTC) registered alongside daily_backup.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is still ~2560 lines — further extraction (reminders/clients/contracts/payments routers) recommended in a follow-up refactor.

reminders.py uses date.today() (server-local timezone) rather than UTC; since scheduler runs at 00:00 UTC = 09:00 Timor, and the container is UTC, this works today. If the container TZ ever changes, day-bucket math would drift — safer to use datetime.now(timezone.utc).date().
The unused `from deps import db as _db` inside run_daily_reminders() (comment says 'avoid circular import' but it's a no-op since deps is already imported at module top). Cosmetic cleanup.

Reminder dedup key is (contract_id, day_bucket, date=today). If the job runs past midnight UTC while a contract stays at bucket=7 for a second calendar day, it could send twice. Acceptable since buckets are 7 and 9 exactly (only 1 UTC day each), but worth documenting.
Consider limiting /api/reminders/logs to last N days (currently .limit(500) with no time filter — will get slow over years).
SettingsIn.reminders_enabled defaults True — good. But the Settings PUT accepts partial payloads (tested here) — confirmed unchanged fields are preserved.
test_credentials: admin@fatinpenhores.tl / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Cashier fixture creates TEST_cashier_iter17@fatinpenhores.tl / cashierpass1 with allowed_modules=['dashboard','payments'].
seed_data_creation: Day-boundary test creates 5 pawn cars + 5 contracts with due_dates 6/7/8/9/10 days ago; contracts are deleted in the finally block. 7- and 9-day contracts leave a reminder_log entry (whatsapp_not_configured -> skipped=1 each). This raised the settings.reminders_last_run_summary.skipped counter to 3 on the live DB — expected/mock-mode behavior. TEST_cashier user remains for future regression runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iter17_reminders.py covers refactor GETs, cashier RBAC, reminders status/run/logs/dedup/toggle/day-boundary, and iter16 disbursement regression. It re-uses admin_session fixture pattern and creates a persistent TEST_cashier user. Run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported (fetch from /app/frontend/.env). The Save button on Settings is the LAST-of-many with text 'Save' — use save_btns[-1]. Reminders card is between WhatsApp Config and Backups.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All iter17 acceptance criteria met.
